title: Pericallis hadrosoma
taxonomy: Pericallis hadrosoma is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This family is commonly known as the bellflower family, consisting of around 90 genera. As a member of the Campanulaceae family, Pericallis hadrosoma is a part of the larger clade of angiosperms, or flowering plants. The plant is native to the Canary Islands and is often found growing in rocky or mountainous regions. Further classification of Pericallis hadrosoma includes its placement in the genus Pericallis, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Macaronesian islands.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems.
